title: Add prediction prompts to 10 key patterns — 'what happens if?' before viewing
description: PRACTICE AUDIT: Zero prediction/self-test features exist (Bloom's Apply = 1/5). Add prediction prompts that appear BEFORE the UML diagram. User reads the prompt, thinks, then clicks to reveal the diagram. EXAMPLES: Observer: 'If you add a 6th observer, how many existing classes change?' (Answer: zero). Strategy: 'Adding Bitcoin payment — how many classes modified?' (Answer: zero). Singleton: 'Can you create 2 instances?' (Answer: no, private constructor). These create the 'testing effect' — active recall dramatically improves retention vs passive viewing.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 patterns have 1-2 prediction prompts each
Prompts displayed in a collapsible 'Think First' card before UML loads
Each prompt has a hidden answer revealed on click
Prompts test the KEY INSIGHT of the pattern (not trivia)
Format: question → user thinks → click reveal → answer + brief explanation

title: Add 'Identify the Pattern' quiz — show UML, user names the pattern
description: PRACTICE AUDIT Bloom's ANALYZE = 0/5. Build a pattern identification quiz: show a UML class diagram from one of the 15 patterns WITHOUT its name, give 4 multiple-choice options, user picks. This tests whether the student can RECOGNIZE pattern structure, not just recall definitions. Uses existing DESIGN_PATTERNS data with name hidden. Randomize distractors from same category for harder questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Quiz accessible from sidebar or bottom panel tab
Shows pattern UML without name, category badge, or description
4 multiple-choice options from same or adjacent categories
Correct: green flash + reveal pattern name + 1-sentence 'the key tell is...'
Wrong: hint ('Look at the relationship types — is it inheritance or composition?')
All 15 patterns in random order, session score at end

title: Add 'Which Pattern?' scenario-based selection challenges
description: PRACTICE AUDIT Bloom's EVALUATE = 0/5. Present a real-world design scenario, user selects the best pattern. EXAMPLES: 'Your app needs to support credit card, PayPal, and Bitcoin payments with different processing logic. Which pattern?' (Strategy). 'A Document goes through Draft→Review→Published states with different behavior in each.' (State). '5 UI components need to coordinate without knowing about each other.' (Mediator). Each scenario has 4 options with explanation of why the correct one is best AND why alternatives are wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: 10+ scenario challenges covering at least 10 different patterns
Each scenario is 2-3 sentences describing a real design problem
4 pattern options per scenario
Correct answer explanation includes WHY this pattern fits
Wrong answer explanation includes WHY this pattern doesn't fit for THIS scenario
Difficulty increases: first 5 are clear-cut, last 5 are ambiguous

title: Add 'SOLID Violation Spotter' practice — find the principle violated in code
description: PRACTICE AUDIT Bloom's ANALYZE + EVALUATE = 0/5. Show a code snippet (10-15 lines) containing a SOLID violation. User identifies which principle is violated. On correct answer, show the before→after UML transformation from existing SOLID_DEMOS data. This is the code-based SOLID quiz from the Concept Quality Audit (LLD-073) but positioned as a PRACTICE feature with scoring and progression.
acceptanceCriteria: 25 code snippets (5 per SOLID principle) in TypeScript
Each snippet is 10-15 lines, readable without deep framework knowledge
5 options per question (one per SOLID principle)
Correct: reveal before→after UML from SOLID_DEMOS + explanation
Wrong: targeted hint pointing at the violation symptom
Running score, streak counter, 'mastered' badge after 20/25 correct

title: Add 'Complete the Diagram' practice — fill in missing classes/relationships
description: PRACTICE AUDIT Bloom's APPLY + CREATE = 0/5. Show a partial UML diagram (e.g., Observer with Subject and Observer interface but ConcreteSubject/ConcreteObserver missing). User adds the missing classes from a palette and connects relationships. System validates against the reference pattern. This is the stepping stone between passive viewing and full blank-canvas design.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 'complete the diagram' exercises for the 5 most important patterns
Each starts with 40-60% of the pattern diagram pre-loaded
User adds missing classes from the Palette tab
User creates relationships between classes
Submit button validates: correct classes present? Correct relationship types?
Feedback shows what's correct (green), what's missing (red), what's extra (yellow)

title: Add Strategy vs State vs Command comparison challenge
description: PRACTICE AUDIT: The #1 confusion in design patterns is Strategy vs State vs Command — they look similar but differ in intent. Build a dedicated comparison exercise: show 3 scenarios, user assigns each to the correct pattern. SCENARIOS: (1) 'Payment method varies by user choice at checkout' → Strategy. (2) 'Document behavior changes as it moves through Draft→Review→Published' → State. (3) 'User actions need to be queued, logged, and undone' → Command. Each wrong answer shows the structural comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: Dedicated comparison challenge in Practice section
3 scenarios with clear-cut answers across Strategy/State/Command
Side-by-side UML diagrams for all 3 patterns shown after completion
Key difference explained: 'Strategy = USER selects, State = SYSTEM transitions, Command = OPERATIONS as objects'
Can be replayed with shuffled scenarios

title: Add scoring system with streak counter and per-concept mastery
description: PRACTICE AUDIT: No scoring, no progress, no feedback loop. Without scores, practice has no motivation. Add: points per correct answer (10 for easy, 25 for medium, 50 for hard), streak counter (consecutive correct), per-concept mastery level (0-5 stars based on correct answers), overall practice score. Persist in localStorage. Display in sidebar header.
acceptanceCriteria: Points awarded for correct quiz/challenge answers
Streak counter visible during practice sessions
Per-concept mastery: 0 stars (no practice) → 5 stars (mastered)
Mastery stars visible next to each pattern/concept in sidebar
Overall practice score displayed in module header
All scores persist across sessions via localStorage

title: Add wrong-answer feedback with visual explanation for all practice modes
description: PRACTICE AUDIT: Practice without feedback is useless. When a student answers wrong, don't just say 'Incorrect.' Show WHY using the visualization. EXAMPLE: Student picks 'State' for a scenario that's actually 'Strategy' → show: 'In State pattern, the SYSTEM decides when to transition (like TCP connection state). In your scenario, the USER chooses the payment method at checkout — that's Strategy. The difference: who initiates the change?'
acceptanceCriteria: Every wrong answer shows a 2-3 sentence explanation of WHY it's wrong
Explanation contrasts the wrong answer with the correct answer
For pattern identification quiz: shows the key structural difference
For scenario challenges: explains the intent mismatch
For SOLID violations: highlights the specific symptom the student missed
